jokes.text: The first time I threw mine away, I thought it was an ant trap.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he threw away radon test kit
jokes.punchline: Mistook safety equipment for ant trap
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's impulsive decision-making and lack of attention to detail. Character-driven but not exceptional.

jokes.text: you know, the things you turn over and it moos like a cow thing
jokes.setup: Michael trying to describe what he thought the radon kit was
jokes.punchline: Hilariously convoluted description of a simple toy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael rambling - overly complicated description of something simple. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: And the third time, I did it out of spite.
jokes.setup: Two accidental throwings established
jokes.punchline: Third time was intentionally petty
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - accident to accident to deliberate spite. Economical and very Michael.

jokes.text: You say radon is silent but deadly and then you expect me not to make farting noises with my mouth?
jokes.setup: Toby describes radon as 'silent but deadly'
jokes.punchline: Michael can't resist the fart joke connection
jokes.explanation: Predictable fart humor setup but delivered with Michael's childlike inability to resist.

jokes.text: And if I had a gun with two bullets, and I was in a room with Hitler, Bin Laden and Toby, I would shoot Toby twice.
jokes.setup: Hypothetical about being trapped with evil historical figures
jokes.punchline: Would shoot harmless Toby instead of terrorists
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office quote. Perfect escalation structure, shocking juxtaposition, and peak Michael hatred for Toby. Became cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: I would kill Bin Laden and then Toby.
jokes.setup: Trying to course-correct after going too far
jokes.punchline: Still puts Toby second to a terrorist
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Michael trying to moderate but still hating Toby more than terrorists.

jokes.text: Curve the bullet like in my favorite James McAvoy film Wanted.
jokes.setup: How to kill multiple people with one bullet
jokes.punchline: References ridiculous action movie physics
jokes.explanation: Andy's earnest reference to absurd movie physics. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: What does Bin Laden... Is there a curtain rod in the room?
jokes.setup: Andy suggests curving bullet around obstacles
jokes.punchline: Jim points out the logical flaw with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim response - logical, deadpan, specific detail that deflates Andy's movie logic.

jokes.text: How about make-believe land has anything you want?
jokes.setup: Jim pointing out logical flaws in scenario
jokes.punchline: Andy gets defensive about his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's childlike investment in the scenario when challenged with logic.

jokes.text: You line them all up, you take one bullet, shoot them all through the throat at the same time.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to kill multiple people with limited bullets
jokes.punchline: Creed has disturbingly practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - disturbing practical knowledge delivered matter-of-factly. Peak character integration.

jokes.text: Phyllis, you're Hitler, come up here. Toby, you're Toby. Andy, you're Bin Laden.
jokes.setup: Creed suggests lining them up to test theory
jokes.punchline: Michael assigns roles, with Toby playing himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - 'Toby, you're Toby' implies he's already evil enough. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: It worked!
jokes.setup: Michael pretending to shoot lined-up coworkers
jokes.punchline: Celebrates success of imaginary mass murder
jokes.explanation: Michael's childlike excitement about dark fantasy. Good payoff but not as strong as setup.

jokes.text: Despera-Dough, Witchy-Womanilla. Why do they do this?
jokes.setup: Eagles-themed ice cream flavors
jokes.punchline: Pam's exasperated reaction to terrible puns
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay made funny by Pam's realistic annoyance. The puns themselves are deliberately bad.

jokes.text: After his last breakup, he ate 40,000 calories in three hours.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're preparing for Michael's heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific number shows Michael's extreme reactions
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd number makes Michael's overeating more ridiculous. Good character insight.

jokes.text: You probably shouldn't keep a baby up that late.
jokes.setup: Pam says she was up all night with baby Cece
jokes.punchline: Oscar misses the point about babies keeping parents awake
jokes.explanation: Classic Oscar - offering unsolicited parenting advice while missing basic reality of infant care.

jokes.text: And if you get stuck and you don't know what to do, make a random sound effect. Okay, farting noise, whatever.
jokes.setup: Instructions for dealing with depressed Michael
jokes.punchline: Farting noises as legitimate distraction technique
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier scene plus Jim's practical approach to Michael's childishness.

jokes.text: Michael, nice tie or something.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to be positive around heartbroken Michael
jokes.punchline: Ryan's half-hearted, uncertain compliment
jokes.explanation: 'Or something' shows Ryan's discomfort and lack of effort. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Cake It To The Limit, that's my favorite.
jokes.setup: Eagles-themed ice cream flavors mentioned earlier
jokes.punchline: Michael likes the most obvious Eagles pun
jokes.explanation: Weak pun made slightly funnier by Michael's enthusiasm for obvious wordplay.

jokes.text: Alleged contract.
jokes.setup: Dwight refers to their child-rearing contract
jokes.punchline: Angela disputes its existence despite detailed discussion
jokes.explanation: Angela's legalistic denial despite obvious existence of contract. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I will do legal jujitsu on you.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening legal action
jokes.punchline: Mixing martial arts metaphor with legal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight - combining his martial arts obsession with legal threats.

jokes.text: See, don't even need to make contact. The law will do it.
jokes.setup: Claiming to do legal jujitsu
jokes.punchline: Extends martial arts metaphor to non-contact legal victory
jokes.explanation: Dwight commits fully to the metaphor with absurd confidence. Good follow-through.

jokes.text: I'd like to see a stool sample.
jokes.setup: Discussing verification of beet juice cleanse
jokes.punchline: Angela demands disgusting proof
jokes.explanation: Angela's prim demand for gross evidence shows her commitment to the contract dispute.

jokes.text: It happened to Tom Hanks in Big.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Michael could mature overnight
jokes.punchline: References movie about child in adult's body
jokes.explanation: Andy's earnest movie reference as evidence. Appropriately naive for character.

jokes.text: Exactly. It happened in Big.
jokes.setup: Andy references Big as precedent
jokes.punchline: Michael agrees with movie logic as real evidence
jokes.explanation: Michael's agreement with Andy's faulty logic shows his childlike reasoning.

jokes.text: Billy Joel Rock Band. That exists?
jokes.setup: Jim offers Billy Joel Rock Band as entertainment
jokes.punchline: Michael's surprise at oddly specific game variant
jokes.explanation: Realistic reaction to absurdly specific game variant. Mild observational humor.

jokes.text: Is it wrong to keep seeing her? Depends on who you ask. I mean, if you ask her husband or you took a random poll, yeah, it's wrong.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if affair is wrong
jokes.punchline: Oscar's matter-of-fact confirmation of obvious wrongness
jokes.explanation: Oscar's dry delivery of obvious moral conclusion. Good structure with 'random poll' detail.

jokes.text: I ask everyone in the room, 'Are you in a relationship?'
jokes.setup: Meredith defending her sexual ethics
jokes.punchline: Her 'ethical' approach to casual sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith - treating promiscuity like a business transaction with minimal ethics.

jokes.text: And in Paris, it is rude for a woman to have less than four lovers.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing Scranton to Paris
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up 'French culture' fact
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael false worldliness - inventing 'sophisticated' cultural norms to justify behavior.

jokes.text: On her diet.
jokes.setup: Michael asks why Stanley can cheat like Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Stanley clarifies Phyllis cheats on diet, not spouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with economy. Two words completely reframe the accusation.

jokes.text: He's a sports guy. He's scummy. Dog fighting, drugs, they spit.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to demonize the husband
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable to ridiculous stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate rationalization through absurd stereotyping. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: Real Sports with Bryant Gumbel.
jokes.setup: Asked where he learned sports guys are scummy
jokes.punchline: Cites TV show as authoritative source
jokes.explanation: Michael treating HBO sports journalism as proof of universal athlete corruption.

jokes.text: My name's Andy Bernard and I am a cuckold.
jokes.setup: Andy in talking head about Michael's situation
jokes.punchline: Overshares his own humiliation with clinical term
jokes.explanation: Andy's self-awareness combined with oversharing and antiquated vocabulary. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You're Ali Larter, I'm Beyoncé. I am Beyoncé always.
jokes.setup: Using movie roles to explain cheating dynamics
jokes.punchline: Kelly's insistence she's always the star
jokes.explanation: Kelly's vanity overriding the actual point of the analogy. Character-driven but predictable.

jokes.text: I can't wait to see this jerk who is making me cheat on his wife. I should punch him in the nose for what he's making me do to her.
jokes.setup: Michael going to confront the husband
jokes.punchline: Blames victim for Michael's own choices
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael logic - victimizing himself while being the actual perpetrator. Excellent irony.

jokes.text: And now coaching third base with two arms, two legs and a heart capable of feeling pain...
jokes.setup: Andy describing the husband they're spying on
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic humanization of ordinary person
jokes.explanation: Andy's theatrical sensibility applied to mundane situation. Very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You were supposed to be the lookout. Yeah, well, it's really warm in here. It's like a sleeping bag.
jokes.setup: Jim and Dwight caught sleeping during work
jokes.punchline: Dwight's childlike defense of falling asleep
jokes.explanation: Dwight's innocent explanation reveals the warehouse sleeping setup absurdity.

jokes.text: I just hate that part of the job, you know? The power dynamic.
jokes.setup: Gabe giving mild reprimand to Jim and Dwight
jokes.punchline: Overthinks simple discipline into psychological analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe - overanalyzing basic management into academic concepts.

jokes.text: I actually designed that chart. Kind of hoping it catches on.
jokes.setup: Gabe mentioning his printer fire decision tree
jokes.punchline: Proud of mundane corporate chart, wants it to go viral
jokes.explanation: Gabe's misplaced pride in bureaucratic work product. Peak middle management delusion.

jokes.text: Finally, there's a provision here in the occurrence that the child is born an old man, a la Benjamin Button.
jokes.setup: Reviewing Angela and Dwight's contract provisions
jokes.punchline: Contract includes fantasy movie scenario
jokes.explanation: Shows how thorough and absurd their contract is. Good absurdist escalation.

jokes.text: ...unless we're unplugged... and wake up in the future.
jokes.setup: Contract discussing Matrix provisions
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of sci-fi scenarios as legal reality
jokes.explanation: Their matter-of-fact discussion of Matrix reality as contract consideration.

jokes.text: I have it buried very deeply, and I don't want to dig past a certain someone to get it.
jokes.setup: Angela claiming she doesn't have $30,000
jokes.punchline: Implies money is buried with/under a body
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark from prim Angela. Excellent misdirection and character subversion.

jokes.text: I bet it's like, 'Hey, Donna, why don't you wash the dishes? High five, Donna.'
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing husband's celebratory high-fives
jokes.punchline: Imagines husband taking credit for wife's chores
jokes.explanation: Michael's projection of selfishness onto innocent person. Character-driven rationalization.

jokes.text: That was a euphemism.
jokes.setup: Michael caught making factual error about coach salaries
jokes.punchline: Claims obvious mistake was intentional wordplay
jokes.explanation: Michael's face-saving lie when corrected. Classic defensive behavior.

jokes.text: Better than most horses.
jokes.setup: Discussion of $30,000 for five sexual encounters
jokes.punchline: Meredith compares to horse breeding rates
jokes.explanation: Peak Meredith - treating human sexuality like livestock breeding with disturbing knowledge.

jokes.text: Those lumps are cats, and those cats have names, and those names are Amber, Milky Way, Dianne and Lumpy.
jokes.setup: Dwight complaining about lumpy bed
jokes.punchline: Angela's detailed defense of cats sleeping in bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela cat lady moment. The escalation and specific names make it funnier.

jokes.text: Do you understand how rare that is in nature?
jokes.setup: Angela demanding eye contact during sex
jokes.punchline: Dwight treats human intimacy like nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - applying wildlife knowledge to human sexuality. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: No, no, but we're gay for baseball.
jokes.setup: Coach asks if they're someone's parents
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate expression of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Andy's oblivious use of potentially offensive slang in awkward context.

jokes.text: I just looked a man in the eyes and I shook his hand. All the time I was thinking, 'I'm sleeping with your wife.'
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on meeting the husband
jokes.punchline: Treats deception as thrilling accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Michael's narcissistic thrill at deception. Shows his complete moral blindness.

jokes.text: You know who does that? James freaking Bond.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his deceptive handshake
jokes.punchline: Compares adultery to spy heroics
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-aggrandizing pop culture comparison. Classic delusion of grandeur.

jokes.text: Well, then, it wouldn't be the first time I stole something away from Coach Shane.
jokes.setup: Michael taking orange slices meant for team
jokes.punchline: Compares fruit theft to stealing man's wife
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel between petty theft and adultery. Good dark humor with character insight.

jokes.text: I'm awful, aren't I? Yeah, that's pretty... I'm an awful guy. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Michael seeking validation for his awfulness
jokes.punchline: Gets excited by confirmation of being terrible
jokes.explanation: Michael's perverse pride in being labeled terrible. Shows his twisted psychology.

jokes.text: That is a dangerous game, friend-o.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he'll eat whatever he wants
jokes.punchline: Creed's ominous warning about eating impulses
jokes.explanation: Creed's mysterious warning implies dark personal experience with impulsive eating.

jokes.text: I don't even know why you're screaming at me right now. I'm not screaming. I'm not screaming.
jokes.setup: Pam and Jim having exhausted parent argument
jokes.punchline: Jim's defensive denial while clearly agitated
jokes.explanation: Realistic portrayal of sleep-deprived parent stress. Relatable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You know what? I think you're attractive and I want to sleep with you. What about Kelly? You read my mind.
jokes.setup: Ryan inspired by Michael's 'take what you want' philosophy
jokes.punchline: Propositions Toby, who assumes he means Kelly
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Toby's misunderstanding. Shows Ryan's shallow adoption of Michael's attitude.

jokes.text: Schrute sperm are strong, but they're no match for a grown Schrute man.
jokes.setup: Dwight planning to sabotage his own fertility
jokes.punchline: Treats sperm vs. man like battle of wills
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - treating biological functions like warfare. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: He don't give an F about nothing.
jokes.setup: Michael not refilling paper dispenser
jokes.punchline: Darryl's admiring assessment of Michael's rebellion
jokes.explanation: Darryl's vernacular appreciation of Michael's petty rebellion. Character-specific language.

jokes.text: Sometimes I think about it when I'm trying to fall asleep at home.
jokes.setup: Describing warehouse sleeping area
jokes.punchline: Dreams of workplace nap spot from actual bed
jokes.explanation: Sad-funny reversal - workplace is more restful than home. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Because a motel is dirty, and it is sexy. Like me and like Donna.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why motel instead of condo
jokes.punchline: Equates himself and lover with dirt/seediness
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional romanticizing of seedy affair setting. Character-driven cringe.

jokes.text: I could have stopped him.
jokes.setup: Michael storms out to meet married woman
jokes.punchline: Jim's flat, matter-of-fact assessment after the fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan. Acknowledges he could intervene but chooses not to. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: Stop kissing me. It's not in the contract.
jokes.setup: Angela and Dwight in compromising position
jokes.punchline: Treating intimacy like contractual obligation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela - reducing human affection to legal terms. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: And it was either living with myself or being happy. And I picked the former.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his moral choice to end affair
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits he chose misery over happiness
jokes.explanation: Michael's accidental honesty about choosing moral suffering. Good misdirection with character insight.

jokes.text: I vow to never listen to my bodily instincts ever again.
jokes.setup: Michael making public apology
jokes.punchline: Promises to ignore all physical needs/desires
jokes.explanation: Michael's typically extreme overcompensation. Classic all-or-nothing thinking.

jokes.text: I'm talking about the Sabre printers that catch on fire. Oh! Okay, I was talking about...
jokes.setup: Michael assumes reporter asking about affair
jokes.punchline: Reporter actually asking about unrelated printer fires
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection ending. Michael's guilty conscience creates humorous misunderstanding.
